perturbation.original_text: All information as stated in Clause 1.2(a) above shall be and remain the sole property of the Discloser and shall be returned to the Discloser forthwith on demand at any time or without demand upon the termination of the other p arty's services .
perturbation.changed_text: All information as stated in Clause 1.2(a) above shall be and remain the sole property of the Discloser. However, electronic information, due to its nature, does not need to be returned to the Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that all information defined in 1.2(a) is the sole property of the Discloser and must be returned upon demand or termination. The added sentence creates an exception for electronic information, directly contradicting the initial statement that ALL information must be returned. This create uncertainty on the status of such electronic information, and if it's now part of the AHPL data, or it isn't, due to the change in the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) All information as stated in Clause 1.2(a) above shall be and remain the sole property of the Discloser. However, electronic information, due to its nature, does not need to be returned to the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The original clause states that all information defined in 1.2(a) must be returned. The changed text adds an exception for electronic information, which directly contradicts the initial all-encompassing statement. This creates uncertainty and inconsistency, because the Change text explicitly creates an exception to the contradicted text's ALL information clause. Thus, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall ensure that none of the patients of AHPL can be identified in any reports, submissions and publications of the Company, which shall be deemed to be Confidential information of AHPL within the meaning of this clause.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company shall make reasonable efforts to ensure that none of the patients of AHPL can be identified in any reports, submissions and publications of the Company, which shall be deemed to be Public information of AHPL within the meaning of this clause.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the protection of patient identity and explicitly defines it as Confidential Information. The modified clause introduces a contradiction by stating that such information is Public information, creating uncertainty and conflicting obligations. If at first the patient's identities were confidential, know they are public, therefore contradicting each other in the same phrase.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) The Company shall make reasonable efforts to ensure that none of the patients of AHPL can be identified in any reports, submissions and publications of the Company, which shall be deemed to be Public information of AHPL within the meaning of this clause.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that patient information is 'Confidential information', while the changed text explicitly defines the same information as 'Public information'. This creates a direct and undeniable contradiction within the same clause, as the definition of patient information changes from confidential to public. This directly contradicts the contradicted text because that also claims the information is public, but the original text defined it as confidential.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing: (i) not to make any commercial use thereof; (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third (3rd) party o ther than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party; and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any information, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one (1) of the exceptions in Clause 1;
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence, except for any electronic copies of the information. Electronic copies may be used internally for process improvement, and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing: (i) not to make any commercial use thereof; (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third (3rd) party o ther than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party; and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any information, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one (1) of the exceptions in Clause 1;
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates maintaining all Confidential Information in confidence. The added exception for electronic copies creates an internal contradiction. The clause now states that these copies may be used internally for 'process improvement,' which is not necessarily aligned with the defined 'Purpose'. This changes the obligation from the one defined at the start of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence, except for any electronic copies of the information. Electronic copies may be used internally for process improvement, and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing: (i) not to make any commercial use thereof; (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third (3rd) party o ther than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party; and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any information, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one (1) of the exceptions in Clause 1;
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to maintaining Confidential Information in confidence, allowing electronic copies to be used for 'process improvement'. This directly contradicts the original clause which mandates that Confidential Information be used 'only for the Purpose and for no other purpose'. Because 'process improvement' is not necessarily aligned with the defined 'Purpose', it creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, as the parties' obligations are no longer consistent. It also weakens the obligation that the initial clause defined, generating contradiction.
